extraction.one_line_summary: GAGNON Filippo won the 32km TT by 2 seconds over VAN AERT Wout, who extended his GC lead despite losing the stage.
tactical_patterns: HATER ran negative splits gaining 13 seconds from checkpoint one to finish, unusual TT pacing strategy paid off.
VAN AERT hit solo speeds 65-67 km/h on flat sections, revealing sprinter-turned-TTer can match pure specialists on power courses.
ROGLIC rode conservatively without attempting to match GAGNON's benchmark, telegraphing he'll wait for high mountains instead.

rider_signals.GAGNON Filippo: positive: destroyed field by 53 seconds over benchmark after deliberately taking stage 3 easy to preserve TT form
rider_signals.HATER Ethan: positive: third place 17 seconds back positions him as unexpected GC threat before mountain stages
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: negative: mechanical with jammed chain cost significant time, GC ambitions now depend entirely on alpine performance
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: deliberately rode for solid time rather than competing with GAGNON, confirming mountain-focused strategy
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: extended yellow by 53 seconds over CATANIA despite losing stage, dual green jersey threat with TT power
